reports.title: Structured Smurfing Network
reports.executive_summary: Three linked accounts (AC-0001, AC-0002, AC-0005) all opened within 5 days of each other are engaged in a coordinated transfer network. 35 transfers from AC-0001 to AC-0002 follow a 4-day cadence with 91% of amounts falling in the $400–$900 structured band and 74% occurring between midnight and 5am. Funds subsequently flow to AC-0005, forming a classic layering chain. Total suspicious exposure is $24,748.

key_evidence.heading: Structured Amount Band
key_evidence.body: 91% of 35 transfers from AC-0001 to AC-0002 fall within $400–$900. This amount range is a known technique to avoid automatic SAR filing thresholds ($1,000+).

key_evidence.heading: Nighttime Concentration
key_evidence.body: 74% of transfers occurred between midnight and 5am. This timing is inconsistent with typical retail transaction behaviour and suggests deliberate scheduling.

key_evidence.heading: Automated Cadence
key_evidence.body: Transfers follow a near-perfect 4-day cadence (strength: 0.83 of 1.0). This regularity is consistent with scripted or automated transfer behavior.

key_evidence.heading: Layering Chain
key_evidence.body: Funds flow AC-0001 → AC-0002 → AC-0005 across three accounts all opened within days of each other in January 2026, consistent with a purpose-built smurfing network.

reports.network_summary: Three accounts form a directed layering chain. AC-0002 acts as the connector node, receiving from AC-0001 and forwarding to AC-0005. All three accounts show near-zero device and IP region variance.
reports.action_summary: Immediately restrict outgoing transfers from all three accounts and escalate to the Tier 2 fraud investigations team. Do not notify account holders until investigation is complete.
reports.limitations: This report is based solely on transaction metadata and agent-detected patterns. It does not prove fraud or establish criminal intent, and does not replace a full human investigation. Patterns identified are probabilistic indicators only.
reports.markdown:
# Fraud Investigation Brief — CASE-001

**RingSight** · Run `RUN-20260607-DEMO` · Generated 2026-06-07

---

## Executive Summary

Three linked accounts (AC-0001, AC-0002, AC-0005) all opened within 5 days of each other are engaged in a coordinated transfer network. 35 transfers from AC-0001 to AC-0002 follow a 4-day cadence with 91% of amounts falling in the $400–$900 structured band and 74% occurring between midnight and 5am. Funds subsequently flow to AC-0005, forming a classic layering chain. Total suspicious exposure is $24,748.

**Risk Score:** 99/100 · **Severity:** Critical · **Action:** Restrict & Escalate · **Urgency:** Immediate

---

## Accounts Involved

AC-0001, AC-0002, AC-0005

---

## Recommended Action

Immediately restrict outgoing transfers from all three accounts and escalate to the Tier 2 fraud investigations team. Do not notify account holders until investigation is complete.

---

## Limitations

This report is based solely on transaction metadata and agent-detected patterns. It does not prove fraud or establish criminal intent, and does not replace a full human investigation.

---

## Analyst Sign-Off

Reviewed by: ____________________  
Date: ____________________  
Decision: ____________________

reports.title: High-Exposure Layering Chain

The consistent 18-day cadence (strength 0.71) and high exposure classify this as a high-risk repeated transfer pair requiring immediate escalation. No merchant category is present — all transactions are classified as internal transfers.

key_evidence.heading: High Total Exposure
key_evidence.body: $83,017 transferred across 14 transactions — significantly above the $50,000 high-exposure threshold. Average transfer size is $5,930.

key_evidence.heading: Repeated Edge
key_evidence.body: The same sender-receiver pair (AC-0010 → AC-0011) is used for all 14 transfers, with no variation in counterparty.

key_evidence.heading: Cadence Pattern
key_evidence.body: 18-day transfer cadence with 71% regularity suggests scheduled or automated transfers rather than ad-hoc behaviour.

reports.network_summary: Two accounts connected by a single high-exposure directed edge. AC-0010 is the sole sender; AC-0011 is the sole receiver. No further downstream accounts are identified in the dataset.
reports.action_summary: Temporarily restrict outgoing transfers from AC-0010 and request source-of-funds documentation for the $83,017 total. Escalate to SAR filing team if no legitimate business purpose is established within 24 hours.
reports.limitations: This report is based solely on transaction metadata and agent-detected patterns. It does not prove fraud or replace a full human investigation. The absence of downstream accounts does not mean funds were not further distributed outside this dataset.
